content: Galathil: I see the Dragonborn’s pet housecarl still clings to him like a cheap tavern wench. Tell me, does your beloved Thane share your… vision of Skyrim, or does he merely tolerate your bleating, like one tolerates the stench of rotten fish at the docks? Lydia: Shut your twisted Bosmer mouth, you sewer-dwelling knife-ears! My Thane appreciates a Nord woman—loyal, fierce, and untainted by Thalmor-loving, tree-fondling weakness. Unlike whatever freak show you’ve sculpted into the mirror today. Galathil: Ah, the mating call of the brainless brute. How charming. I sculpt , you dull-witted milk-drinker. While you’re busy grunting and swinging that overcompensating sword, my patrons slink from my chair as entirely new people—smarter, prettier, and far less likely to die in a ditch defending some fool’s misguided prejudices. Lydia: Ha! Your “art” is just hacking up faces for cowards and sneak-thieves. Meanwhile, I’ve split more Thalmor skulls than you’ve had hot meals. And my Thane? me—every night, in every way. Bet your wrinkled hands can’t say the same.

Galathil: How... quaint. Enjoy your barbaric rutting while it lasts, housecarl. Sooner or later, even brute warriors grow tired of brute companions. Lydia: Hah! Keep carving faces in your piss-stinking cistern, corpse-toucher.
